vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1430: Deacon runs false-merged check on closed-out issues, gets repeated 401s from GitHub App token (probably expired credential)

plan.title: Deacon runs false-merged check on closed-out issues, gets repeated 401s from GitHub App token (probably expired credential)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The deacon's false-merged reconciler (`reconcileFalseMerged` in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts) iterates every review-status row with mergeStatus='merged' and calls the GitHub App to verify the PR is actually merged. It does NOT consult the issue's tracker state, so issues that have already been closed out (state=closed, label=closed-out) are still polled every 60s. When the GitHub App credentials become invalid (revoked, uninstalled-from-repo, or installation-id rotated), every poll throws a 401 from the patrol, spamming the log indefinitely. The retry behavior in `githubApi` (src/lib/github-app.ts) is a single fetch with no retry and no circuit breaker, so transient or persistent 401s burn rate budget and pollute logs identically.
narratives.Proposal: Three narrowly-scoped fixes: (1) Add a canonical-state pre-filter to `reconcileFalseMerged` that uses the existing autoCloseOut canonical-state cache pattern (10-min TTL, single `gh issue view --json state,labels` call). Skip issues whose canonical state is `done` or `canceled` — there is nothing to reconcile once the lifecycle is over. (2) Modify the private `githubApi` helper in github-app.ts to detect HTTP 401 and retry exactly once after re-issuing the installation token via `generateInstallationToken`. (3) Add a module-level circuit breaker in github-app.ts: when a retried 401 still fails, mark the App degraded for 5 minutes and log ONE actionable error pointing at the diagnostic recovery path (`node scripts/create-github-app.mjs`). While degraded, `githubApi` fails fast without making a network call; the flag self-clears after the TTL with a single re-enable log.

autoDecisions.summary: Bundle implementation and unit tests in the same bead per behavior (3 beads total instead of 6)
autoDecisions.rationale: Splitting impl from tests would create un-shippable beads (impl with no coverage in main briefly); the 'one focused change, independently mergeable' rubric is best served by impl+test pairs.

autoDecisions.summary: Use a module-level circuit breaker over a per-issue throttle
autoDecisions.rationale: The user's symptom is 'spamming the same 401 every patrol cycle' — a global breaker stops the rate-budget bleed for ALL App-backed calls, not just the reconciler. A per-issue throttle would still spam if N issues 401.

autoDecisions.summary: 5-minute breaker TTL with self-clearing single re-enable log
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches 5 patrol cycles (60s each), giving the operator a window to fix credentials before the next attempt. Self-healing avoids requiring a manual reset command for a transient issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope the retry/breaker to the private `githubApi` helper only
autoDecisions.rationale: The reported symptom path goes through `githubApi` (false-merged uses getPullRequestState → githubApi). The other direct-fetch sites (mergePullRequestWithAppPromise, reportCommitStatus) are out of the reported scope and not part of the patrol cadence that causes the log spam. Consistency across all sites is a reasonable follow-up but not required by the acceptance criteria.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse the existing autoCloseOutCache canonical-state lookup pattern, not a new shared helper
autoDecisions.rationale: The autoCloseOut reconciler already established the cached-`gh issue view` pattern at deacon.ts:3766 with a 10-min TTL. Inlining a similar lookup in `reconcileFalseMerged` keeps the diff small and the cache key (issueId) shared with the existing cache rather than introducing a second source of truth.

autoDecisions.summary: Diagnostic recovery command in error message is `node scripts/create-github-app.mjs`
autoDecisions.rationale: The script exists and is the established re-setup path. The issue hints at `pan admin github-app refresh` but no such command currently exists; pointing at the actual script is the truthful actionable hint.

items.title: Skip closed/canceled issues in reconcileFalseMerged patrol

narrative.Action: In `reconcileFalseMerged` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:2917), before calling `getPullRequestState`, look up the issue's canonical tracker state using the same cached `gh issue view --json state,labels` pattern as `getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState` (deacon.ts:3766). Skip issues whose canonical state is `done` or `canceled` (i.e., the GitHub issue is closed). Reuse or share the existing `autoCloseOutCache` so the canonical-state lookup is shared across patrols, not re-queried per reconciler. Cover the new filter with a unit test in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-false-merged-scope.test.ts.

subItems.title: `reconcileFalseMerged` skips issues whose tracker canonical state is `done` (closed without canceled label)
`reconcileFalseMerged` skips issues whose tracker canonical state is `canceled`
Active-pipeline issues (in_progress, in_review, verifying_on_main) are still checked — behavior preserved
The canonical-state lookup is cached (10-min TTL or shared autoCloseOutCache) — closed issues do not cause a new `gh issue view` call every 60s patrol
Unit test in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-false-merged-scope.test.ts` verifies closed/canceled issues are not polled (no GitHub App call made)
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

items.title: Retry githubApi once after re-issuing installation token on HTTP 401

narrative.Action: In the private `githubApi<T>` helper (src/lib/github-app.ts:142), detect HTTP 401 responses. On 401, call `generateInstallationToken` (the existing Effect-based generator) again to obtain a fresh installation token and retry the same request exactly once with the new token. Only retry on 401 — do not retry on any other status code. If the retry also returns 401, throw the same error the function currently throws (the circuit-breaker bead handles persistent failure). Add a unit test that mocks `fetch` and verifies the retry path.

subItems.title: On HTTP 401 from `githubApi`, `generateInstallationToken` is called again and the request is retried exactly once with the new token
If the retry succeeds (200/204), the caller receives the parsed response — no exception
If the retry also returns 401, the original error-throwing path runs (caller sees the 401 surface area unchanged from today, modulo the breaker bead)
Non-401 errors (403, 404, 5xx) do NOT trigger a retry — the existing single-shot error path is preserved
Unit test in `src/lib/__tests__/github-app-401-retry.test.ts` covers the 401→regenerate→200 success path and the 401→regenerate→401 failure path
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

items.title: Module-level circuit breaker after retried 401 with single actionable log

narrative.Action: In src/lib/github-app.ts, add module-level state (`appDegradedUntil: number`, `appDegradedLogged: boolean`) and helpers `markAppDegraded(reason)` / `isAppDegraded()`. When the token-retry-on-401 retry path still returns 401, call `markAppDegraded` with the actionable diagnostic message: 'GitHub App credentials invalid (persistent 401 after token re-issue). Disabling App-backed calls for 5 minutes. To recover: `node scripts/create-github-app.mjs` (PANOPTICON_DEV_WEBHOOKS=1).'. While `isAppDegraded()` returns true (5-min TTL), `githubApi` throws immediately without a network call. The error message points at the same diagnostic command. When the TTL expires, log ONCE that App calls are re-enabled and clear `appDegradedLogged`. Unit-test that the breaker opens after a retried 401, stays open for the TTL, blocks `githubApi` calls in the meantime, and re-opens after expiry.

subItems.title: After token-retry-on-401 fails with a second 401, `markAppDegraded` is called with the diagnostic recovery command in the message
While degraded, `githubApi` throws immediately with the actionable error message and DOES NOT make a network call
The degraded-mode entry log is emitted EXACTLY ONCE per breaker open — repeated 401s while open do not re-log the same message
Breaker auto-clears after 5 minutes; the re-enable log is emitted exactly once when the breaker closes
While the breaker is open, the deacon's false-merged patrol completes its cycle without hammering GitHub — the per-issue 401 log spam is eliminated

Unit test in `src/lib/__tests__/github-app-circuit-breaker.test.ts` covers: breaker opens after retried 401; subsequent calls fail fast; only one log per open; auto-clears after TTL (use vi.useFakeTimers / vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync per the project rule on retry tests)
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass
